mechanism_narrative: A stable or declining FEDFUNDS rate over the trailing 3 months signals absence of tightening pressure, lowering the equity discount rate and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This is an always-on trend effect independent of regime.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS held at 2.41% since Dec 2018 hike, Fed signaled pause in early 2019
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX return remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-rate-change periods do not show significantly lower returns (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by 0.2 percentage points or more over a rolling 3-month window from a cyclical low, it signals an inflection in labor market strength historically associated with late-cycle turning points, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as markets price recession risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.2pp over trailing 3 months from a cyclical low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% in March 2019, near multi-decade low
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger fires, SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) jointly signal broad-based consumer and housing demand deceleration that is not captured by either series alone, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM decline AND HOUST MoM decline in same reporting month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage fell from 1162 to 1121 thousand units, RSAFS revised down from 505969 to 491751
failure_condition: Joint-decline instances produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term's marginal contribution to Rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from the sum of individual series' IC (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and corporate earnings capacity, translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets incorporate near-term production strength.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value 110.475 reported Feb 2019 vintage
failure_condition: Correlation between INDPRO MoM change and forward 5-day SPX return is not significantly different from zero (p>0.10) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where FEDFUNDS has plateaued and CPIAUCSL growth remains subdued (below 2.5% YoY), the combination signals a 'goldilocks' environment of paused tightening and low inflation risk, jointly producing stronger positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days than either signal alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS flat (+/-0.1pp) over 3 months AND CPIAUCSL YoY < 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage 254.148 vs revised 254.277 in March 2019, implying subdued inflation; FEDFUNDS steady at 2.41%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with paused monetary tightening
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods do not outperform single-signal (FEDFUNDS-only) periods by a statistically significant margin (t-test p>0.05) over any 3-year window, or Rank IC of the interaction falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in PAYEMS growth below its trailing 12-month average pace signals weakening labor demand, historically preceding broader economic slowdown, and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as forward earnings expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average MoM change falls below 50% of trailing 12-month average pace
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised down from 150816 to 150295 thousand persons in March 2019 vintage, indicating hiring deceleration
failure_condition: Triggered instances yield positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive quarter-over-quarter growth in GDPC1 reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as an always-on structural trend signal.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 revised from 18765.256 to 20304.874 billion dollars reflecting large benchmark revision
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 QoQ growth and forward 60-day SPX return is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or high GDPC1-revision-risk periods systematically invert the sign of the relationship.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising-rate regime, a decline in HOUST combined with a rising FEDFUNDS trajectory jointly signal that higher borrowing costs are actively suppressing housing demand, a leading indicator of broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days that exceed what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: HOUST MoM decline AND FEDFUNDS higher than 6-month-ago level
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST fell from 1162 to 1121 thousand units following 2018 rate hikes to 2.41%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances fail to show more negative average 5-day returns than the HOUST-only decline signal (difference not significant at p<0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.
